Subject: NedBank Account Statement - 18-09-22
Body: Click here to read more Dear Customer Welcome to the movement towards a cleaner, greener, paperless world. Attached to this email is your encrypted electronic statement with enhanced security. To open it simply follow the step-by-step instructions below. Also, please advise us if your email address changes that way we can ensure that you receive your eStatement safely, on time, every time. By opting for eStatements, youre making a tangible contribution to shrink your, and Nedbanks, carbon footprint. As South Africas green bank, and to show our commitment to a cleaner, greener world, Nedbank will donate 25c to The Green Trust for every eStatement we send. Its the small things we do together that add up to make a big difference in curbing climate change. Opening your encrypted statement To open your statement you will need Adobe Reader version 5 or higher. Click here to download. Step 1: Double-click on the attachment. Step 2: Type in your password. Step 3: Click 'OK'. This message has been digitally signed to enable you to verify both its origin and integrity. If the message has been tampered with in any way, a security warning will alert you when you open the mail. To verify the senders digital identity simply click on the red ribbon. Your Password Your password is your account number. To help you avoid confusion if you have multiple current accounts, simply look at the name of the attachment in the email the last four digits are the same as the last four digits in your account number. However, please be sure that you use your full account number when typing in your password(s). Statement Cycle Nedbank will email your statement in accordance with your chosen statement cycle (eg monthly). If we encounter a problem delivering your eStatement eg mailbox is full we will continue trying for up to 24 hours. Thereafter the email will be deemed undeliverable. If your statements remain undelivered for three consecutive cycles, Nedbank will cease to send you eStatements. To resume the service, simply contact Nedbank on 0860 555 111 and ensure that your eStatements delivery details are up to date. For more information or if you need assistance with your eStatement call 0860 555 111 between 07:30 and 18:00 Monday to Friday and 7:30 and 13:00 on Saturdays. eNotes Sign up for Nedbanks new active SMS update service ... more... eStatements Choose to have your current account statements delivered straight to your email inbox ... more... Subject: Secrets of Invalidating Tax Assessments June 18
Body: UNCOVER THE STRATEGIES, TACTICS AND REMEDIES IN INVALIDATING TAX ASSESSMENT Hereunder is the list of our Upcoming Training Program: How to Prepare Performance Evaluation Forms and Performance Improvement Plans (PIP) - June 17 Reg. Link: https://forms.gle/wynM5hZ7zsYFGgJo7 Invalidating Tax Assessments вҖ“ June 18 Reg. Link: https://forms.gle/n3JRJHB4jGqjybWP8 Supervisory Leadership Development вҖ“ June 20 Reg. Link: https://forms.gle/YfuXNAhKHumQAVut5 Modern Procurement and Supply Management вҖ“ June 25 AM Reg. Link: https://forms.gle/X3umJ9houjAsh18n9 Warehouse Management Best Practices вҖ“ June 25 PM Reg. Link: https://forms.gle/nKyTrPLLUmeNqaDq8 Work Attitude and Values Enhancement вҖ“ June 27 AM Reg. Link: https://forms.gle/tEuhroo2PV11TPaG9 Accounting For Non-Accountants вҖ“ June 27 PM Reg. Link: https://forms.gle/mndSfjB3kXSWmVJU8 Invalidating Tax Assessments UNCOVER THE STRATEGIES, TACTICS AND REMEDIES IN INVALIDATING TAX ASSESSMENT June 18, 2025 2:00pm - 5:00pm *LIVE VIA ZOOM* Introduction: Are you or is your business being assessed (or likely to be assessed) by the B.I.R.? Have you received any notice (Letter of Authority, Tax Verification Notice, Subpoena Duces Tecum, and the like)? Are you prepared? You need to be aware of what exactly you need to do or you will stand to spend an arm and a leg. Uncover the strategies, tactics, and remedies in handling tax assessments, disputes, and claims for refunds. Course Objectives: This course is designed specifically for individuals and businesses currently facing tax assessments by the Bureau of Internal Revenue (B.I.R.) or those who anticipate an impending assessment. Participants will gain practical insights into strategies for addressing tax assessments, contesting erroneous claims, and pursuing tax refunds. By the end of the course, participants will be empowered to effectively manage and navigate their tax challenges, minimizing financial and legal risks. Major Takeaways: Develop strategies to respond to B.I.R. assessments and potential disputes. Recognize critical assessment stages and appropriate reactions to notices received. Identify common audit findings and learn how to effectively challenge them. Understand the tax refund process and the necessary steps for successful claims. Course Content Assessing Your Tax Assessment Situation a. Legal basis of authority to assess Taxpayer b. Prescriptive period of Tax Assessments * 3 years vs 10 years prescriptive period * Extension of period to assess through the issuance of waiver c. Types of BIR audit/investigation notices * Letter of authority, Letter Notice, Tax Verification Notice and Mission Order * Service of assessment notices d. Immediate Steps to Take Upon Receiving a Notice * Key actions to undertake to protect your interests. e. Submission of documents pursuant to BIR notice * Issuance of subpoena duces tecum * Application of the best evidence rule Understanding the Stages of Tax Assessment a. Notice of discrepancy (NOD) b. Preliminary Assessment Notice (PAN) Best practices for responding to a PAN to avoid a final assessment. c. Final Assessment Notice (FAN) What to do when you receive a FAN and how to build your case. d. Final Decision Disputed Assessment (FDA) Strategies for navigating the final resolution process and implications for your business. Challenging Common Audit Findings a. Questioning the validity of tax assessment at different stages of tax assessment Overview of frequent discrepancies found during audits and how they affect your assessment. b. Addressing factual and legal issues in tax assessment Crafting compelling arguments and gathering supporting documentation to challenge the assessment. Claiming Tax Refunds a. Understanding Refund Claims and Their Requirements Types of claims: Erroneously paid taxes versus overpaid input VAT. Identifying the prescriptive periods for claims and key documentation requirements. b. Rule on exhaustion of administrative remedies Knowing the procedural steps necessary to pursue your rights effectively. Featured Speaker: MR. SENEN QUIZON Tax Partner - Deloitte Philippines Senen has over 17 years of professional experience in tax services. His experience covers tax ruling assistance, tax investigation assistance, tax training, and tax consultancy for companies engaged in the Manufacturing, Life Sciences & Health Care, and Technology, Media & Telecommunications industries. Prior to joining Deloitte, he was the Chief of the Direct Taxes Branch of a government agency, where he conducted numerous studies in the area of direct tax and tax administration. During his stint in the government, he participated in the legislative process by drafting proposed tax legislations and serving as a resource person in public hearings on various tax measures. Senen is an Accredited Tax Practitioner of the Bureau of Internal Revenue and is a regular speaker at the Philippine Institute of Certified Public Accountants (PICPA). He worked for 10 years in another local accounting firm as part of its Tax Knowledge Center, which handles training and publications. He earned his degree in Bachelor of Science in Applied Economics from the Polytechnic University of the Philippines and received his MasterвҖҷs degree in Business Administration from Colegio de San Juan de Letran. He has also attended a variety of foreign tax training programs sponsored by JICA, IMF, and other multilateral agencie LEARNING INVESTMENT Invalidating Tax Assessments UNCOVER THE STRATEGIES, TACTICS AND REMEDIES IN INVALIDATING TAX ASSESSMENT June 18, 2025 2:00pm - 5:00pm *LIVE VIA ZOOM* REGISTRATION LINK: https://forms.gle/Qytikc5ycoMRE6ra8 ONLINE PER PAX Regular Rate Php 1,899.00 + VAT starting JUNE 12, 2025 Early Bird Rate Php 1,499.00 + VAT until JUNE 11, 2025 Group Rate Php 1,399.00 + VAT per pax for 5 вҖ“ 14 pax Super Group Rate Php 999.00 + VAT per pax for 15 or more pax BIRTHDAY MONTH PROMO - ONE (1) FREE SEAT Register within Your Birthday Month with at least 2 paid pax @Php1,899.00+ VAT per pax Must Present proof of date birth JOIN NOW PAY LATER PROMO - After 30 Days from the date of the Masterclass @Php2,099.00 + VAT per pax Inclusive of the Webinar Session, Presentation Materials and the Digital Certificate ATTENTION TO MS. JHIE For More Details, Inquiry or Reservation Please Call Us On: Ms. Jhie Marketing Officer EmailAddress: jhie.pmxtraining@gmail.com Tele Fax: 8470-1057 or Call/Text: 0906-4798186 Visit my FB PAGE @jhie.training You are receiving this email because you have opted into one of our sites or attended one of our events. In-House Training We can conduct the following in-house training for your company to make them more customized to your requirements: * Human Resource or HR * Labor Law * Taxation * Accounting * Supply Chain Management * Leadership, Management, Supervision * Soft Skills * Sales * Customer Service * Collection Want to change how you receive this email?manage preference В· unsubscribe

Subject: 20 impeachment counts against Texas AG
Body: It could be a quick end for Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton. Advertisement eNewspaper | Crosswords | Horoscopes Read in browser Read in browser The Daily Briefing YOUR MORNING NEWS ROUNDUP Fri May 26 2023 Nicole Fallert | Newsletter Writer 20 impeachment counts against Texas AG It could be a quick end for Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton. Advertisement A Texas House committee has recommended to impeach embattled Attorney General Ken Paxton amid a series of allegations of abuse of office. Also in the news: An Indiana doctor who spoke out about providing a 10-year-old rape victim with abortion care was found liable for violating patient privacy laws. Washington is heading out for the long weekend with mere days until the nation could see an unprecedented default on the national debt. рҹҷӢрҹҸјвҖҚвҷҖпёҸ I'm Nicole Fallert, Daily Briefing author. Are you reading this from the airport? Today's the busiest day for flying in years. Now, here we go with Friday's news. 20 impeachment counts against Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton Texas lawmakers have issued 20 articles of impeachment against state Attorney General Ken Paxton, ranging from bribery to abuse of public trust as state Republicans surged toward a swift and sudden vote that could remove him from office. The charges were released Thursday night, hours after a Republican-led House investigative committee recommended impeaching the stateвҖҷs top lawyer. The House could vote on the recommendation as soon as Friday. If it impeaches Paxton, he would be forced to leave office immediately. Read more Indiana doctor who reported Ohio 10-year-oldвҖҷs abortion violated privacy laws An Indianapolis doctor who spoke publicly about providing abortion care to a 10-year-old rape victim from Ohio was found liable Thursday for violating state and federal patient privacy laws by the Indiana Medical Licensing Board. During a disciplinary hearing, Dr. Caitlin Bernard said she followed state reporting requirements and hospital policy вҖ” which she has asserted repeatedly. But in a split decision, the board found that the doctor violated privacy laws in her handling of the patientвҖҷs information. While Bernard could have had her license revoked, the board found her fit to continue practicing medicine. Read more вҖўSouth Carolina's six-week abortion ban is now law. Here's what's next.вҖў An 11-year-old Mississippi boy who called 911 was shot by the responding officer. Dr. Caitlin Bernard testifies on Thursday, May 25, 2023, during a hearing in front of the state medical board. Michelle Pemberton/IndyStar More news to know now вҖўOath Keepers founder Stewart Rhodes was sentenced to 18 years for the Jan. 6, 2021, Capitol attack. вҖўBrian Laundrie's mother wrote him letter saying she would help ''dispose of a body.''вҖўHurricane season is going to be вҖҰ well, pretty normal.вҖў A shark bit off the leg of a 22-year-old U.S. woman snorkeling off Blue Haven Resort in Turks and Caicos.вҖўCarmelo Anthony retires, presidential campaigns and Tina Turner's legacy. What happened in this week's news? вҖўOn today's 5 Things podcast, USA TODAY Photojournalist Josh Morgan discusses the ''Flags In'' ceremony to honor American troops at Arlington National Cemetery. Listen on Apple Podcasts, Spotify, or your smart speaker. What's the weather today? Check your local forecast here. Six days until the debt ceiling deadline President Joe Biden and lawmakers prepare to leave Washington for the long Memorial Day weekend with only days remaining until a June 1 expiration date for the nation's debt. Unless a deal is reached Friday, negotiators are expected to continue to meet virtually over the Memorial Day weekend. House Republicans began departing the capital Thursday, but are on a 24-hour notice from McCarthy to reconvene at the Capitol if necessary. House Democrats tried to seize on Republicans' absence: With one side of the chamber empty, Democratic Leader Hakeem Jeffries called the debt ceiling crisis "manufactured, MAGA madness" during remarks from the House floor. Read more вҖўBiden nominated the first Black chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff since Colin Powell. вҖўExclusive interview: Nikki Haley talks about the 2024 GOP path to victory.вҖўMexico's president knocked DeSantis, says Hispanic voters shouldn't give him ''a single vote.'' US House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries, Democrat of New York, in Washington, DC, May 25, 2023. SAUL LOEB, AFP via Getty Images Supreme Court limits government power to curb water pollution The Supreme Court on Thursday sided with a couple who have been battling the Environmental Protection Agency for more than a decade over a plan to develop a property in the Idaho panhandle. The decision has potentially sweeping national ramifications for water quality, agriculture and development. вҖўA majority of the court said that the lack of clarity about what types of waters are covered by the Clean Water Act was difficult for property owners to understand.вҖўWhat Alito wrote: A "staggering array of landowners are at risk of criminal prosecution" if they don't realize their property is subject to federal environmental rules.вҖўBottom line: The court's opinion adopts a new, more narrow standard that will limit how much water the EPA may regulate. That's a win for developers and a loss for environmentalists. Read our full recap of the decision here and how the court sided with a grandmother who lost her home. Head of Russian private army says forces are handing control of Bakhmut to Moscow The head of the Russian private military contractor Wagner claims his forces have started pulling out of Bakhmut in eastern Ukraine and handing over control to the Russian military. His announcement Thursday came days after he said Wagner troops had captured the ruined city. The Bakhmut triumph delivered a badly needed victory for Russian President Vladimir Putin, whose invasion of Ukraine in February 2022 has lost momentum and now faces a Ukrainian counteroffensive using advanced weapons that KyivвҖҷs Western allies have provided. A new book tracks the mystery.вҖў Bourbon & truffles? Maker's Mark adopts puppy to hunt truffles at its distillery farm. These articles are for USA TODAY subscribers. You can sign up here. Study identifies symptoms that distinguish long COVID A clearer picture of what's become known as long COVID-19 is starting to emerge, which should eventually allow researchers to treat symptoms that can devastate people's lives for months or years after a COVID-19 infection. In a new study funded by the National Institutes of Health, researchers identified symptoms that are the most distinctive to long COVID, including: fatigue, especially after exercise, brain fog and dizziness, among others. Researchers believe it's possible that long COVID has different causes in different people, or that some people are affected by a combination of factors. Read more вҖўMapping Colorado River water: How California, Arizona and Nevada plan to use less. 20 impeachment counts against Texas AG

It could be a quick end for Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton. Advertisement eNewspaper | Crosswords | Horoscopes Read in browser Read in browser The Daily Briefing YOUR MORNING NEWS ROUNDUP Fri May 26 2023 Nicole Fallert | Newsletter Writer 20 impeachment counts against Texas AG It could be a quick end for Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton. Advertisement A Texas House committee has recommended to impeach embattled Attorney General Ken Paxton amid a series of allegations of abuse of office. Also in the news: An Indiana doctor who spoke out about providing a 10-year-old rape victim with abortion care was found liable for violating patient privacy laws. Washington is heading out for the long weekend with mere days until the nation could see an unprecedented default on the national debt. рҹҷӢрҹҸјвҖҚвҷҖпёҸ I'm Nicole Fallert, Daily Briefing author. Are you reading this from the airport? Today's the busiest day for flying in years. Now, here we go with Friday's news. 20 impeachment counts against Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton Texas lawmakers have issued 20 articles of impeachment against state Attorney General Ken Paxton, ranging from bribery to abuse of public trust as state Republicans surged toward a swift and sudden vote that could remove him from office. The charges were released Thursday night, hours after a Republican-led House investigative committee recommended impeaching the stateвҖҷs top lawyer. The House could vote on the recommendation as soon as Friday. If it impeaches Paxton, he would be forced to leave office immediately. Read more Indiana doctor who reported Ohio 10-year-oldвҖҷs abortion violated privacy laws An Indianapolis doctor who spoke publicly about providing abortion care to a 10-year-old rape victim from Ohio was found liable Thursday for violating state and federal patient privacy laws by the Indiana Medical Licensing Board. During a disciplinary hearing, Dr. Caitlin Bernard said she followed state reporting requirements and hospital policy вҖ” which she has asserted repeatedly. But in a split decision, the board found that the doctor violated privacy laws in her handling of the patientвҖҷs information. While Bernard could have had her license revoked, the board found her fit to continue practicing medicine. Read more вҖўSouth Carolina's six-week abortion ban is now law. Here's what's next.вҖў An 11-year-old Mississippi boy who called 911 was shot by the responding officer. Dr. Caitlin Bernard testifies on Thursday, May 25, 2023, during a hearing in front of the state medical board. Michelle Pemberton/IndyStar More news to know now вҖўOath Keepers founder Stewart Rhodes was sentenced to 18 years for the Jan. 6, 2021, Capitol attack. вҖўBrian Laundrie's mother wrote him letter saying she would help ''dispose of a body.''вҖўHurricane season is going to be вҖҰ well, pretty normal.вҖў A shark bit off the leg of a 22-year-old U.S. woman snorkeling off Blue Haven Resort in Turks and Caicos.вҖўCarmelo Anthony retires, presidential campaigns and Tina Turner's legacy. What happened in this week's news? вҖўOn today's 5 Things podcast, USA TODAY Photojournalist Josh Morgan discusses the ''Flags In'' ceremony to honor American troops at Arlington National Cemetery. Listen on Apple Podcasts, Spotify, or your smart speaker. What's the weather today? Check your local forecast here. Six days until the debt ceiling deadline President Joe Biden and lawmakers prepare to leave Washington for the long Memorial Day weekend with only days remaining until a June 1 expiration date for the nation's debt. Unless a deal is reached Friday, negotiators are expected to continue to meet virtually over the Memorial Day weekend. House Republicans began departing the capital Thursday, but are on a 24-hour notice from McCarthy to reconvene at the Capitol if necessary. House Democrats tried to seize on Republicans' absence: With one side of the chamber empty, Democratic Leader Hakeem Jeffries called the debt ceiling crisis "manufactured, MAGA madness" during remarks from the House floor. Read more вҖўBiden nominated the first Black chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff since Colin Powell. вҖўExclusive interview: Nikki Haley talks about the 2024 GOP path to victory.вҖўMexico's president knocked DeSantis, says Hispanic voters shouldn't give him ''a single vote.'' US House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries, Democrat of New York, in Washington, DC, May 25, 2023. SAUL LOEB, AFP via Getty Images Supreme Court limits government power to curb water pollution The Supreme Court on Thursday sided with a couple who have been battling the Environmental Protection Agency for more than a decade over a plan to develop a property in the Idaho panhandle. The decision has potentially sweeping national ramifications for water quality, agriculture and development. вҖўA majority of the court said that the lack of clarity about what types of waters are covered by the Clean Water Act was difficult for property owners to understand.вҖўWhat Alito wrote: A "staggering array of landowners are at risk of criminal prosecution" if they don't realize their property is subject to federal environmental rules.вҖўBottom line: The court's opinion adopts a new, more narrow standard that will limit how much water the EPA may regulate. That's a win for developers and a loss for environmentalists. Read our full recap of the decision here and how the court sided with a grandmother who lost her home. Head of Russian private army says forces are handing control of Bakhmut to Moscow The head of the Russian private military contractor Wagner claims his forces have started pulling out of Bakhmut in eastern Ukraine and handing over control to the Russian military. His announcement Thursday came days after he said Wagner troops had captured the ruined city. The Bakhmut triumph delivered a badly needed victory for Russian President Vladimir Putin, whose invasion of Ukraine in February 2022 has lost momentum and now faces a Ukrainian counteroffensive using advanced weapons that KyivвҖҷs Western allies have provided. Subject: Real estate is Gen ZвҖҷs new side hustle
Body: First Up Plus: Why are so many condo elevators broken? вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ вҖҢ LIVING HERE By Amber Shortt Good afternoon. IвҖҷm Amber Shortt, the StarвҖҷs housing editor. HereвҖҷs the latest on broken condo elevators, young investors turning to real estate and when to list your house. GOOD WEEK FOR... Housing advocates. A new report says TorontoвҖҷs post-pandemic oversupply of office space could last for decades, bolstering arguments to turn the space toward residential buildings. Recent homebuyers. Long maligned, the mortgage stress test is getting credit for blunting pain among new owners as rates jumped 4.25 percentage points over the space of 15 months. Mortgage brokers. Many fixed-rate fans are opting for a three-year term over the slightly lower five-year fixed rate in anticipation of possible lower rates next year. Where should you eat next? If youвҖҷre wondering where to eat as the city heats up, you need Food Crawl, the StarвҖҷs free food newsletter. Whether itвҖҷs a strip mall standout or a downtown gem, Food Crawl will make you an insider on the best eats in Toronto and beyond. Sign up for free here. BAD WEEK FOR... The city. Toronto is suing developers for allegedly failing to repay the $200,000 cost of protecting a shelter from neighbouring condo construction вҖ” claims the developers deny. This landlord. A man was sentenced to 20 years in prison for allowing a tenant to set up an opioid drug lab in the basement of his Pickering rental home, while a family unwittingly rented upstairs. Residents on the 40th floor. The near monopoly of elevator installation and repair in Toronto means plenty of condo elevators are out of service. HereвҖҷs what the regulatorвҖҷs doing. TRENDING It used to be that when young people graduated school, they were consumed with finding that first job. Now, increasingly, theyвҖҷre looking to nab that first investment property, too. A recent survey for Royal LePage found more than a quarter of Canadians who had yet to jump into the investment market planned to do so within the next five years, with about half of them between the ages of 18 and 34. And theyвҖҷre willing to couch surf to do it. The survey found 33 per cent of younger investors bought their first income property before a home to live in themselves, either while renting or living with family. TheyвҖҷre also the most likely of any cohort to buy more than one income property, often pairing with a financing partner, be it family, friend or other, to afford it. And who can blame them? Considering, for example, the average cancer researcher makes roughly $57,000 a year вҖ” just over half the required $100,000 income to comfortably lease a one-bedroom apartment in Toronto, and barely enough to meet the $55,000 salary to uncomfortably rent one вҖ” it wouldnвҖҷt be surprising if todayвҖҷs youth see real estate as more lucrative than their day job. It's a notion that was floated by Ben Rabidoux, president of market research firm North Cove Advisors, back in 2021 at the height of the pandemic market when speculation purchases were running rampant. вҖңI donвҖҷt know how many people who would have ended up in the STEM field are instead flipping houses. ItвҖҷs so lucrative right now you canвҖҷt blame them, but I donвҖҷt know if, from a longer-term perspective, that enhances productivity in our country,вҖқ he told the Star at the time. It seems Gen Z and millennial investors are just taking a page out of the boomer playbook. Those aged 55 and older were vastly overrepresented among real estate investors in Ontario, making up 57 per cent despite being just 32 per cent of the population, a recent Statistics Canada report showed. For them, the investment has paid off вҖ” whether that will repeat for todayвҖҷs young investors remains to be seen. вҖңIf you go back over 65 years, the real estate market continues to outperform many of the other indices вҖ” the TSX or the NASDAQ,вҖқ Mike Heddle of Royal LePage State Realty told the StarвҖҷs Tess Kalinowski. But another report, this time from CIBC and Urbanation, warns that many investors who bought pre-construction condos just a few years ago are now underwater, as rising interest rates and costs have put half of condo investments in the red. Still, after watching everyone from firefighters to chefs to nurses not be able to afford to both work and live in Toronto, it seems the cityвҖҷs blistering housing market may have turned the old adage of вҖңdonвҖҷt quit your day jobвҖқ for your side project on its head. Now, if you want a day job, be it teaching high school or curing cancer, the advice might be вҖңdonвҖҷt quit your realtor.вҖқ LOCATION, LOCATION,LOCATION NEWS FROM AROUND THE GTA AND BEYOND For those who want to get away from it all while still having it all close by, this $700,000 Wasaga cottage is a five-minute drive from a Walmart. Check it out. Residents along Eglinton Avenue face seemingly endless construction вҖ” and they live on the second-worst road in Ontario. These other roads made the list. CanвҖҷt afford a house in Toronto? Maybe you can buy a castle in Monaco вҖ” or any of these other glamorous properties cheaper than local real estate. HereвҖҷs what the creators of the new book Denison Avenue have to say about the history and current state of TorontoвҖҷs Chinatown and Kensington Market. Japanese researchers made a house made partially of shredded disposable diapers. HereвҖҷs why this breakthrough is the s---. [Science News] Function doesnвҖҷt trump form in this 10,000-square-foot King home, where all you see is вҖңthe really pretty things.вҖқ Look inside. SHOULD I LIST MY HOME NOW OR WAIT TILL THE FALL? ADVICE FROM THE EXPERTS With prices rising but the traditionally slow days of summer real estate on their way, the Star talked to One Group broker Nasma Ali about whether itвҖҷs best to list a home now or wait until the fall market. HereвҖҷs what she said: Whether you decide to sell now or wait until fall depends on your circumstances, and thereвҖҷs advantages to both. If you have already purchased a home to move into, or a tenant has moved out and you'd like to avoid vacancy months, then selling now is best. In the summer, families with children are also trying to buy and settle down before the start of the new school year. However, summer activity becomes exceptionally slow in August вҖ” specifically the last half of August when everyone is scrambling to enjoy the last days of sunshine and vacation. If you get to August and you have time to wait, then it may be worth waiting until after Labour Day. My advice would be to be patient and list the second week of September, as there tends to be a flood of listings that first week. Waiting lets you observe any new sale benchmarks or records set and price accordingly. The fall market offers you a small window of prime activity, until around mid October. After that, activity becomes more steady. Have a burning real estate question of your own? Email us at livinghere@thestar.ca. Thanks for reading. Living Here will be back in your inbox next week. For them, the investment has paid off вҖ” whether that will repeat for todayвҖҷs young investors remains to be seen. вҖңIf you go back over 65 years, the real estate market continues to outperform many of the other indices вҖ” the TSX or the NASDAQ,вҖқ Mike Heddle of Royal LePage State Realty told the StarвҖҷs Tess Kalinowski. But another report, this time from CIBC and Urbanation, warns that many investors who bought pre-construction condos just a few years ago are now underwater, as rising interest rates and costs have put half of condo investments in the red. Still, after watching everyone from firefighters to chefs to nurses not be able to afford to both work and live in Toronto, it seems the cityвҖҷs blistering housing market may have turned the old adage of вҖңdonвҖҷt quit your day jobвҖқ for your side project on its head. Now, if you want a day job, be it teaching high school or curing cancer, the advice might be вҖңdonвҖҷt quit your realtor.вҖқ LOCATION, LOCATION,LOCATION NEWS FROM AROUND THE GTA AND BEYOND For those who want to get away from it all while still having it all close by, this $700,000 Wasaga cottage is a five-minute drive from a Walmart. Check it out. Residents along Eglinton Avenue face seemingly endless construction вҖ” and they live on the second-worst road in Ontario. These other roads made the list. CanвҖҷt afford a house in Toronto? Maybe you can buy a castle in Monaco вҖ” or any of these other glamorous properties cheaper than local real estate. HereвҖҷs what the creators of the new book Denison Avenue have to say about the history and current state of TorontoвҖҷs Chinatown and Kensington Market. Japanese researchers made a house made partially of shredded disposable diapers. HereвҖҷs why this breakthrough is the s---. [Science News] Function doesnвҖҷt trump form in this 10,000-square-foot King home, where all you see is вҖңthe really pretty things.вҖқ Look inside. SHOULD I LIST MY HOME NOW OR WAIT TILL THE FALL? ADVICE FROM THE EXPERTS With prices rising but the traditionally slow days of summer real estate on their way, the Star talked to One Group broker Nasma Ali about whether itвҖҷs best to list a home now or wait until the fall market. HereвҖҷs what she said: Whether you decide to sell now or wait until fall depends on your circumstances, and thereвҖҷs advantages to both. If you have already purchased a home to move into, or a tenant has moved out and you'd like to avoid vacancy months, then selling now is best. In the summer, families with children are also trying to buy and settle down before the start of the new school year. However, summer activity becomes exceptionally slow in August вҖ” specifically the last half of August when everyone is scrambling to enjoy the last days of sunshine and vacation. If you get to August and you have time to wait, then it may be worth waiting until after Labour Day. My advice would be to be patient and list the second week of September, as there tends to be a flood of listings that first week. Waiting lets you observe any new sale benchmarks or records set and price accordingly. The fall market offers you a small window of prime activity, until around mid October. After that, activity becomes more steady. Have a burning real estate question of your own? Email us at livinghere@thestar.ca. Thanks for reading. Living Here will be back in your inbox next week.
